refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment of teleoperation data rate.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control logic
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data flow
coupling_check.notes: The adaptive protocol logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions allow for adaptive protocol effectiveness.
Risk that error correction overhead may introduce latency impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency constraints explicitly in FR refinement to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send status and packet loss metrics to monitoring components.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control, less suitable for communication protocol logic.
Highest containment and ownership fit for communication quality management.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety policies, not communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if packet loss impacts safety alerts.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by addressing packet loss through adaptive communication protocol.

diffusion_risks: Minor risk of communication logic spreading into NGHE Solution or Vehicle modules if not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and aligns well with DP responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit latency constraints in FR refinement.
Define interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety-critical communication.
Establish monitoring and alerting mechanisms for packet loss breaches.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding latency constraints and clarifying safety interface contracts will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and safety communication requirements explicitly.

assumptions: Network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Adaptive communication protocol can meet packet loss targets without excessive latency.
open_risks: Interface integration risks with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance testing data on packet loss and latency under various network conditions.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
